comments.author: @Essahgyabin
comments.text: Indeed a ride in a public bus in Guyana is like a carnival, the sound system alone resembles that of a spot/bar. I was shocked to note that no one in the bus complained about the loud music. so I had to also remain silent and adjust. As a Ghanaian, I believe life in Guyana is closer to that of my motherland, Ghana. I was thrilled by a ride in one of their speed boats plying along the Essequibo river highway to the interior. God Bless Guyana, and Ghana.

comments.author: @elettner
comments.text: You have to understand Geography to understand the Guyanas and the North of Brazil. It all depends on the rivers, seasons and the rain. One can't build anywhere one wants. The Amazon River deposits huge amounts of sediment over the State of Amapa, and the Guyanas. There is no way of building close to the shore. It's all mangrove forest. A true fortification. No big port can exist there. But also, nobody can build a paved road there neither.

comments.author: @weedaniel9463
comments.text: The floating hotel shown in the video at 25:55 is not in Guyana.  It is the Uiara Amazon Resort, a floating hotel located on the Rio Negro in Manaus, Brazil.  Some portions of the jungles are also from Central Brazil.  The real wet lands/ forests in Guyana are much more beautiful than shown in this video.  There are a lot of nice videos (including this one) about Guyana, but clearly there is a lot of need to get the best from this great country.
